building_row.open_questions: Is any qualifying studio rear-facing rather than directly exposed to Sunset, what are the full recurring mandatory charges beyond base rent, how secure is the garage and elevator access in current practice, and what is the current normal approval timeline?
building_row.notes: Current web evidence keeps 7950 West Sunset viable on amenities and current pricing but not as a clean tour-first choice. Official and listing sources confirm controlled access, concierge or package handling, pool, gym, in-unit laundry, central AC, cat-friendly terms, and active guided tours. The main problem is location noise: the building sits directly on Sunset Boulevard in a very active retail and nightlife corridor, Apartments.com shows SoundScore 68 with traffic and businesses marked busy, and Redfin labels the location high noise. Review signals are mixed and stale: a Yelp search result shows a sizable 3.5 rating corpus, but older syndicated review excerpts describe security lapses, overnight fire alarms, odor complaints, and sharp rent increases. Pricing is only partly transparent because official floor-plan disclosures say displayed rents exclude variable mandatory charges and city rent tax. That combination supports research_complete rather than tour_candidate.

evidence_rows.claim: Official and major listing sources describe controlled access, concierge or package handling, pool, roof terrace, fitness amenities, in-unit washer or dryer, and central air conditioning.
evidence_rows.quote_or_note: Official site advertises top tier amenities and guided tours; Apartments.com lists package concierge, concierge, controlled access, pool, roof terrace, fitness center, washer/dryer, and AC.

evidence_rows.claim: Location noise risk is materially elevated because the building fronts Sunset Boulevard in an active business corridor.
evidence_rows.quote_or_note: Apartments.com shows SoundScore 68 with traffic and businesses marked busy; Redfin labels the location high noise.

evidence_rows.claim: Displayed rents do not include variable mandatory charges, so the real monthly fee stack is not self-serve clear.
evidence_rows.quote_or_note: Official disclaimer says displayed prices do not include variable mandatory charges, insurance, utility services, city mandated rent tax, or optional add-ons.

evidence_rows.claim: Management signal is mixed: current marketing and listing pages show an active onsite leasing operation, but older syndicated review excerpts describe security and maintenance frustrations.
evidence_rows.source_name: ForRent review excerpts and Yelp search result
evidence_rows.quote_or_note: ForRent surfaces older complaints about poor security, overnight fire alarms, odor issues, and rent hikes, while Yelp search shows a 3.5 rating from 62 reviews.

evidence_rows.claim: Current Stage 2 pricing remains attractive for the target window and pets are allowed with disclosed monthly cat rent and deposit.
evidence_rows.quote_or_note: Zillow shows current studio inventory now from 2349 to 2451 and discloses cat policy with 25 monthly cat rent and 250 cat deposit.

packet_markdown:
# 7950 West Sunset building packet

## Snapshot
- Building ID: 7950-west-sunset-7950-w-sunset-blvd
- Address: 7950 W Sunset Blvd, Los Angeles, CA 90046
- Primary URL: https://7950westsunset.com/
- Management company: Legacy Partners, Inc.
- Recommended status: research_complete
- Last updated: 2026-04-05

## Current Stage 2 Unit Context
- Unit(s) reviewed: 227
- Current pricing observed: Studio 227 at 2490 base rent, available now; Zillow also showed other current studios around 2349 to 2451
- Immediate unit-specific open questions: exact exposure for unit 227, whether there is meaningful street noise spill into the stack, and the real recurring charge stack beyond base rent

## Scorecard
- Quiet: 2 (medium, evidence count 3)
- Management: 3 (medium, evidence count 4)
- Amenity: 4 (high, evidence count 4)
- Community stability: 3 (low, evidence count 2)
- Location fit: 4
- Pricing transparency: 3

## Risks
- Structural noise risk: medium
- Location noise risk: high
- Security risk: medium
- Pricing risk: medium

## Evidence summary
- [official] Official site and Apartments.com support controlled access, concierge or package handling, pool, roof terrace, gym, in-unit laundry, and central AC. Source: 7950 West Sunset and Apartments.com. Note: active guided tours plus package concierge and property manager on site.
- [review] Apartments.com and Redfin both point to meaningful external noise risk. Source: Apartments.com and Redfin. Note: SoundScore 68 with traffic and businesses marked busy, plus Redfin high noise label.
- [official] Official pricing disclosures are incomplete for all-in budgeting. Source: floor plan page. Note: displayed rents exclude variable mandatory charges, city rent tax, insurance, and utilities.
- [review] Resident sentiment is mixed and somewhat stale. Source: ForRent and Yelp search results. Note: older complaints mention break-ins, alarms, and odor issues; Yelp search still shows a sizable review corpus.
- [official] Current pricing and pet terms are still competitive. Source: Zillow building page. Note: current studios now around 2349 to 2451 and cat policy is disclosed.

## Open questions
- Is there any rear-facing or courtyard-oriented qualifying studio worth prioritizing over a Sunset-facing unit?
- What are the mandatory recurring charges and city taxes in a real quote sheet?
- Has garage and elevator security improved relative to older complaint patterns?
- What is the current normal approval timeline from completed application to decision?

## Notes
7950 West Sunset remains a plausible budget-friendly fallback because the live studio pricing is strong for the submarket and the amenity stack is solid. The issue is that almost every current external signal points to a noisier-than-ideal address. With quiet as the top priority and with only mixed operational review evidence, this is better treated as researched but not yet tour-worthy unless a protected, rear-facing stack can be confirmed and the fee sheet comes back clean.
